mechanism_narrative: Persistently near-zero FEDFUNDS levels sustained for multiple months compress the equity risk premium by lowering the discount rate applied to future cash flows, producing a structural positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the following quarter as investors reallocate from cash and bonds into equities.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.12% as of 2009-12-01 vintage, near zero lower bound
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns during sustained near-zero FEDFUNDS periods are not significantly higher than the unconditional historical mean (t-test p > 0.05), or rank IC stays below 0.03 across rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in UNRATE of at least 0.2 percentage points measured over a trailing 3-month window signals a turning point in the labor market that precedes broad-based earnings recovery, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month as forward earnings expectations are revised upward.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls by >= 0.2pp over trailing 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE revised to 9.9% for 2009-12-01, down slightly from 10.0% vintage
failure_condition: Following triggered UNRATE declines, 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of instances, or the hit-rate does not exceed the unconditional 65.5% positive base rate at p > 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month acceleration in INDPRO and PAYEMS confirms a genuine broad-based economic recovery rather than a statistical bounce, and this joint confirmation drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month because it reduces uncertainty about the durability of the recovery relative to either signal alone.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO MoM > 0 AND PAYEMS MoM > 0 concurrently
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage 100.30 vs revised 88.38 reflecting large historical revision risk
failure_condition: Joint INDPRO+PAYEMS positive signals produce 20-day forward return hit-rates statistically indistinguishable from either single-series signal alone (chi-square test p > 0.10), invalidating the interaction premise.

mechanism_narrative: In the early phase of a post-recession recovery, a sustained rebound in HOUST above its recent trough signals renewed household demand and construction activity that feeds through into broader consumption and equity valuations over the following quarter, producing positive S&P 500 returns specifically during this regime.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST revised to 588K for 2009-11-01, still near historic lows
failure_condition: During non-recovery regimes (e.g., mid-cycle expansion or tightening), the same HOUST rebound signal shows no positive excess return or shows a reversed sign, and even within the target regime rank IC < 0.03 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is pinned near the zero lower bound and CPIAUCSL month-over-month inflation turns negative or near-zero, deflationary risk signals emerge that raise real interest rates and compress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week as markets price in disinflation risk.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL revised value 217.347 vs vintage 217.541 for 2009-12-01
regime_hint: zero lower bound with disinflation risk
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following the joint trigger are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the effect disappears once excluding 2008-2009 crisis observations from the estimation sample.

mechanism_narrative: A monotonic increase in PAYEMS over three consecutive monthly readings acts as an always-on momentum signal for economic expansion, and this labor market momentum is positively associated with S&P 500 forward returns over the next month regardless of the broader macro regime.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS vintage 130910K vs revised 129808K for 2009-12-01, high revision risk noted
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS 3-month momentum and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or the signal underperforms a naive random-walk baseline in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth (RSAFS) occurring while UNRATE remains elevated indicates resilient consumer spending despite labor market weakness, a divergence that surprises markets positively and produces short-horizon positive S&P 500 returns as it signals underlying demand strength ahead of labor market lag.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS revised 338736M vs vintage 352985M for 2009-12-01, UNRATE at 9.9% revised
failure_condition: The joint RSAFS-strong/UNRATE-high signal fails to outperform the RSAFS-alone signal in 5-day forward return hit-rate by a statistically significant margin (p > 0.10), refuting the interaction claim.

mechanism_narrative: During the early-cycle recovery regime immediately following a recession trough, a sustained rise in INDPRO over consecutive quarters signals capacity utilization recovery that supports corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter specifically in this regime and not in late-cycle or contraction regimes.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO showing recovery trend into December 2009 vintage of 100.30
failure_condition: In non-early-cycle regimes, the same INDPRO uptrend signal produces statistically indistinguishable or negative 60-day forward returns compared to baseline, or within the target regime rank IC < 0.03 over rolling windows.
